When using pull-to-refresh on list pages (DishesPage, HistoryPage, WeekPage), the action causes the user to be logged out and redirected to the login screen instead of refreshing the data.

This appears to be a critical bug affecting the core pull-to-refresh functionality that was recently implemented in the Mobile UX Enhancements.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Log in to the app on a mobile device
2. Navigate to any list page (Dishes, History, or Week)
3. Pull down to trigger pull-to-refresh
4. Observe unexpected logout

Expected behavior:
- Data should refresh
- User should remain on the same page
- User should remain authenticated

Actual behavior:
- User is logged out
- User is redirected to login screen

Possible causes:
- Pull-to-refresh onRefresh handler may be calling wrong API
- Auth token may be getting cleared
- Error in refetch logic causing auth failure
- React Query mutation may be affecting auth state
close_reason: Fixed by removing hard redirect from API client and adding error handling to pull-to-refresh. Now handles 401 errors gracefully through auth state instead of forcing navigation.

## Context
- Fastify API in `apps/api/src/`
- SQLite + Drizzle ORM. Schema: `apps/api/src/db/schema.ts`
- Migrations: `mise exec -- pnpm --filter @dinner-planner/api db:generate` then `mise exec -- pnpm --filter @dinner-planner/api db:migrate`

- Use `mise exec -- <command>` (NOT `bash -i -c`)

## Work

### 1. Schema (`apps/api/src/db/schema.ts`)
Add after the `prepTasks` table:
```typescript
export const dishNotes = sqliteTable('dish_notes', {
  id: text('id').primaryKey(),
  dishId: text('dish_id')
    .notNull()
    .references(() => dishes.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }),
  note: text('note').notNull(),
  createdById: text('created_by_id').references(() => users.id),
  createdAt: text('created_at').notNull(),
});
```
(No updatedAt — notes are immutable, delete-only.)

### 2. Migration

`mise exec -- pnpm --filter @dinner-planner/api db:migrate`

- `createDishNoteSchema` — `{ note: z.string().min(1).max(2000) }`
- `dishNoteSchema` — `{ id, dishId, note, createdById: z.string().nullable(), createdByUsername: z.string().nullable(), createdAt }`
- Export types: `CreateDishNoteInput`, `DishNote`

title: bug: serving size scale should be per dinner entry, not a grocery list display filter
description:
## Current behavior
The scale selector (1×/2×/4×) lives on the GroceryPage and applies a display-time multiplier to all grocery items at once. It resets on navigation.

## Expected behavior
Scale should be set per planned dinner entry — e.g. 'We're making chicken casserole for guests this week → 2×'. That entry's ingredients are then included in the grocery list at 2× quantity. Other dishes are unaffected.

## Why this matters
The current approach conflates 'I want to see everything doubled' with 'I'm actually making this dish for more people'. Grocery quantities need to reflect reality at list-generation time, not at display time.

## Design
- Add a `scale` integer column (default 1) to `dinner_entries` table (migration required)
- PATCH /api/entries/:id — accept `scale` field
- `getWeekGroceries` in menus.ts multiplies ingredient quantities by entry.scale when building the list
- Remove the scale selector from GroceryPage
- Add a scale control to the dish planning UI (WeekPage / TodayPage) next to each planned entry — small segmented 1×/2×/4× control inline with the dish card
- Custom grocery items and standing items are not scaled (they are not recipe-derived)
- Tests: migration snapshot, service unit test for scaled quantities, route test, frontend for scale control on WeekPage

When building IngredientSource list, multiply ingredient quantity by the entry scale. title: Adding a dish doesn't persist to database
description:
When creating a new dish through the UI, the dish appears to be added but doesn't persist to the database. After refresh or navigation, the dish is no longer visible.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Dishes page
2. Click to add a new dish
3. Fill in dish details (name, description, etc.)
4. Submit the form
5. Observe dish appears in the list
6. Refresh page or navigate away and back
7. Observe dish is no longer in the list

Expected behavior:
- Dish should be saved to database
- Dish should persist across page refreshes
- Dish should be visible after navigation

Actual behavior:
- Dish appears temporarily in UI
- Dish is not persisted to database
- Dish disappears on refresh

Possible causes:
- Create mutation not calling API correctly
- API endpoint not saving to database
- Database transaction not committing
- Optimistic update without proper server sync
- React Query cache issue not triggering actual API call
- Missing or incorrect API route handler
close_reason: Root cause: z.coerce.boolean() in dishQuerySchema converted string 'false' to boolean true (any non-empty string is truthy). GET /api/dishes?archived=false was returning archived=true dishes instead, so the list always appeared empty. Fixed with z.preprocess to compare string === 'true'.

## Bug Fix: Frontend timezone — date pages show UTC date instead of local date

### Root cause
`new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]` always returns the **UTC** date. At 9:55 PM EST (UTC-5), UTC is already Saturday, causing the Today page to show Saturday while it's still Friday locally.

### Fix: use local date methods

The browser's `Date` object local methods (`getFullYear`, `getMonth`, `getDate`) always return values in the user's **local timezone** (whatever timezone the browser is set to). No configuration needed — just stop using `toISOString()` for date-only strings.

### Add a shared utility

Add a `localDateStr(date?: Date): string` function to `apps/web/src/lib/utils.ts`:

```typescript
export function localDateStr(date?: Date): string {
  const d = date ?? new Date();
  return `${d.getFullYear()}-${String(d.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')}-${String(d.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')}`;
}
```

### Fix these 4 files

**`apps/web/src/pages/GroceryPage.tsx`** (line 10-12):
Replace:
```typescript
function getTodayDateStr(): string {
  return new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
}
```
With:
```typescript
function getTodayDateStr(): string {
  return localDateStr();
}
```
(import `localDateStr` from `@/lib/utils`)

**`apps/web/src/pages/TodayPage.tsx`** (around line 28):
Find the function/expression that uses `toISOString().split('T')[0]` for today's date and replace with `localDateStr()`. (Read the file first to see the exact pattern.)

**`apps/web/src/pages/WeekPage.tsx`** (around line 49):
Find the `formatDate` or similar function that uses `toISOString().split('T')[0]` and replace with `localDateStr(date)`. (Read the file first.)

**`apps/web/src/pages/PantryPage.tsx`** (lines 17, 21):
Replace `new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]` and `soon.toISOString().split('T')[0]` with `localDateStr()` and `localDateStr(soon)`.

**Add `.github/workflows/release.yml`** — a `workflow_dispatch` release workflow:

```yaml
name: Release

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version:
        description: 'Version to release (e.g. v1.2.3)'
        required: true
        type: string

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  release:
    name: Create Release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Validate version format
        run: |
          if ! echo "${{ github.event.inputs.version }}" | grep -qE '^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$'; then
            echo "Invalid version format. Use v1.2.3"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Bump version in package.json
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ github.event.inputs.version }}"
          # Strip leading v for package.json
          npm version ${VERSION#v} --no-git-tag-version --workspaces=false

      - name: Commit and tag
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add package.json
          git commit -m "chore: release ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}"
          git tag ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}
          git push origin HEAD:main --follow-tags

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          gh release create ${{ github.event.inputs.version }} \
